### What is a Markov chain? (A second attempt at the first question)

A Markov chain is a mathematical model that describes a sequence of events where the probability of the next event depends only on the current event, not on the sequence of events that occurred before it. This is known as the "one-step memory" property.

A **positive matrix** (or positive matrix) is a matrix where every single element is strictly greater than zero.

*   **Definition:** $A \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}$ is positive if $a_{ij} > 0$ for all $i, j$.

A **non-negative matrix** (or non-negative matrix) is a matrix where every single element is greater than or equal to zero.

*   **Example:**
    $$\begin{pmatrix} 2 & 0 \\ 0 & 5 \end{pmatrix}$$
    This is a non-negative matrix because $2, 0, 0, \text{ and } 5$ are all greater than or equal to zero.

A **stochiatic matrix** (or stochastic matrix) is a square matrix whose rows (and columns, if it is also column-stochiatic) sum up to one.
